steps.title: Eliminate x by aligning coefficients
steps.content:
Notice that the $x$-coefficient in equation 2 is exactly double that in equation 1: $10x = 2(5x)$. Multiply equation 1 by 2:
$$10x + 28y = 90$$
Subtract equation 2 from this result:
$$(10x + 28y) - (10x + 7y) = 90 - 27$$
$$21y = 63$$
$$y = 3$$

steps.title: Back-substitute to find x and compute xy
steps.content:
Substitute $y = 3$ into equation 2:
$$10x + 7(3) = 27$$
$$10x + 21 = 27$$
$$10x = 6$$
$$x = 0.6$$
Therefore, the product is $xy = 0.6 \times 3 = 1.8$.
